macro_summary: Markets enter the week with a packed catalyst calendar: June CPI prints Tuesday July 14 alongside Fed Chair Warsh's Congressional testimony, setting the tone for the July 28-29 FOMC meeting where markets currently price ~25% odds of a hike. The semiconductor supercycle narrative remains dominant, with TSMC reporting Wednesday July 16 in what could serve as a sector-wide read-through for NVDA, AAPL, and the broader AI trade. Tech megacaps are broadly dislocated from analyst consensus targets, creating a backdrop where earnings-driven re-rates are the primary thesis across multiple screening runs.
screening_note: 22 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. All 6 qualifying tickers cleared this bar, with 4 appearing in 15+ of 22 runs. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: July 13: SKHY permanent ticker begins trading on US exchanges
July 14 8:30 AM ET: June CPI release — headline expected soft/negative MoM on energy base effects, core sticky ~2.9%
July 14: Fed Chair Warsh Congressional testimony (same day as CPI)
July 16: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings — consensus expects EPS $3.80 vs $2.47 YoY (+54%), revenue ~$40B
July 28-29: FOMC rate decision (~25% market-implied hike probability)

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 22 overnight screening runs — the strongest consensus signal of the session. SK Hynix's ADR transitions to its permanent US ticker today (July 13), completing a debut that priced at $149, opened at ~$168-170 (~13-14% first-day pop), and was 7x oversubscribed. The structural bull case is durable: SK Hynix is the dominant HBM supplier to NVIDIA and Apple, HBM supply is sold out through most of 2027, and the CEO has publicly stated the global memory shortage could persist past 2030 — a multi-year demand floor. Revenue grew 46.76% in 2025 with earnings up 116.89%, and global chip sales were up 104% YoY in May 2026.
ideas.catalyst: Permanent SKHY ticker begins first full trading week July 13, 2026; Q2 2026 earnings expected next month; no US-listed direct comparable at this scale.
ideas.edge: IPO priced at $149, closed day-1 at ~$168-170 (+13-14%); 7x oversubscribed with no price discovery exhaustion yet from index buying; HBM supply sold out through 2027+; no US-listed direct comp of comparable scale. Options market likely illiquid in first week — stock-only positioning preferred until options market matures.
ideas.bull_case: Structural AI memory supercycle with CEO-confirmed shortage visibility past 2030, dominant market position in HBM with NVDA and AAPL as anchored customers, and first-week price discovery on a new permanent listing with index inclusion potential ahead.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO lock-up expiry and early price discovery risk could create near-term volatility; options unavailable for defined-risk positioning; geopolitical South Korea/US trade tensions could pressure the ADR; macro rate repricing on Tuesday CPI could compress growth multiples broadly.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 21 of 22 overnight runs with the highest high-conviction vote count of any idea (16 high). Despite 40% Azure YoY growth, Microsoft trades ~25% below its 52-week high at ~$383, its cheapest forward multiple (21-22x NTM earnings) since 2023. The analyst consensus gap is glaring: 55 analysts carry an average price target of ~$589, implying ~56% upside from current levels. Q4 FY2026 earnings on July 29 — the same day as the FOMC decision — create a binary catalyst where Azure guidance and AI capex commentary are the swing factors. A defined-risk call spread into the print is the preferred structure across runs.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 (coincides with FOMC rate decision day); Fed Chair Warsh testimony July 14 as a macro tone-setter.
ideas.edge: ~56% gap between current price (~$383) and average analyst target (~$589); 55 analysts covering with consensus buy; forward multiple at 21-22x NTM — near a decade low for MSFT despite 40% Azure growth. Defined-risk call or call spread limits binary earnings/FOMC exposure.
ideas.bull_case: Azure growing 40% YoY while the stock sits at its cheapest valuation in years — any earnings beat or upward AI capex guidance could trigger a significant multiple re-rate from a highly oversold position relative to analyst consensus.
ideas.bear_case: July 29 FOMC decision lands same day as earnings, creating a double binary; if the Fed signals a hike or Azure growth guidance disappoints, the stock could see further compression despite cheap optics; AI capex narrative remains contentious with profitability scrutiny.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 21 of 22 runs with the highest proportion of high-conviction votes (19 of 21). Goldman Sachs notes NVDA's forward P/E sits at 21.7x versus its 5-year average of 72x — a historically extreme discount for a company posting $75.2B in data center revenue last quarter (+85% YoY) with HBM supply sold out through 2027. 68 analysts carry an average target of $302.73 versus a current price of ~$202, a ~49% gap. TSMC's July 16 earnings are the critical near-term sector catalyst: a beat-and-raise from TSMC would be a direct positive read-through for NVDA demand. The hard NVDA catalyst is earnings confirmed August 26 AMC — a September expiry call spread gives runway past both events.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings July 16 (direct sector read-through); NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 AMC.
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of 21.7x vs. 5-year avg of 72x (Goldman Sachs); 68 analysts with avg target $302.73 vs. ~$202 current (~49% upside); stochastic oscillator in oversold zone; data center revenue +85% YoY. September call spread provides runway past both TSMC and NVDA earnings events.
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beat on July 16 validates AI chip demand and acts as a rising tide for NVDA; NVDA's own August 26 earnings could re-rate the stock toward the $302 analyst consensus as the forward P/E discount to history closes.
ideas.bear_case: Export control escalation or a TSMC guidance miss could pressure the semis tape hard; NVDA stock is up only 4.5% YTD suggesting market skepticism; a hawkish CPI/FOMC combination compresses growth multiples before earnings provide a floor.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 15 of 22 runs as a near-term earnings setup with a clear July 30 catalyst. The stock has pulled back ~9% over the past month to ~$284 against a consensus analyst target of ~$315 (~11% upside), creating a defined entry into what has been 8 consecutive quarters of EPS beats. The $30B Broadcom AI chip deal announced July 10 anchors the AI on-device narrative, Services revenue hit an all-time high of $30.98B/quarter, and a $100B buyback remains active. Rumored fall launch of 5 new iPhones including a $2,500 foldable adds a product cycle catalyst beyond the earnings print.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; $30B Broadcom AI chip deal announced July 10; fall foldable iPhone launch cycle building.
ideas.edge: Stock ~9% off recent highs vs. consensus target of $315 (~11% upside gap); 8 consecutive EPS beats; Services at all-time high $31B/quarter; $100B buyback active. Aug expiry call spread offers defined risk into earnings vs. holding stock at 37x P/E.
ideas.bull_case: 8-quarter EPS beat streak, fresh AI supply deal with Broadcom, all-time high Services revenue, and an active buyback create strong support; a foldable iPhone reveal ahead of fall could add a product supercycle premium to the multiple.
ideas.bear_case: AAPL trades at a premium 37x earnings versus NVDA at ~30x, limiting re-rate upside; China sales remain a geopolitical risk; July 30 earnings fall one day after MSFT and the FOMC, meaning macro fatigue and competitor positioning could mute any positive reaction.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: TSM appeared in 7 of 22 runs, all 7 with high conviction — zero medium votes. TSMC reports Q2 2026 earnings on July 16 (4 days away) with consensus expecting EPS of $3.80 versus $2.47 YoY (+54%) and revenue of ~$40B versus ~$30B YoY (+32%). Prediction markets give a 94.5% probability of a consensus beat, Citi has placed the stock on a 30-day upside catalyst watch, and TSMC has beaten estimates in each of the last 4 quarters with an average +8.34% positive surprise. Monthly revenue data has already largely de-risked the print. A strong result would be a rising-tide read-through for NVDA, AAPL, and the broader AI semis trade.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report confirmed July 16, 2026 — 4 days away; Citi 30-day upside catalyst watch active.
ideas.edge: Average analyst target $520 implies ~17% upside from ~$444 current; prediction markets 94.5% beat probability; CEO pre-guided ~30% full-year revenue growth in USD; 4-quarter beat streak with avg +8.34% positive EPS surprise. Defined-risk call spread expiring late July preferred over outright stock into binary print.
ideas.bull_case: Monthly revenue data has already de-risked the quarterly number; AI chip demand described as 'extremely robust' by management; a beat-and-raise could re-rate TSM to $520 analyst consensus while simultaneously lifting the broader semis complex.
ideas.bear_case: Taiwan geopolitical risk is a persistent overhang that can overwhelm fundamentals; any guidance softening on 2H capacity or AI customer pull-forward concerns could pressure the stock despite a headline beat; options carry earnings premium.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 7 of 22 runs, all 7 with medium conviction, functioning as a macro hedge or tactical binary trade rather than a directional conviction call. Tuesday July 14 delivers a double-barreled macro event: June CPI at 8:30 AM ET and Fed Chair Warsh's Congressional testimony on the same day. Headline CPI is expected soft-to-negative MoM on energy base effects, but core CPI is sticky at ~2.9%. With 25% market-implied July hike probability and 61% September hike probability, a hotter-than-expected core print could reprice odds sharply and compress growth multiples, while a soft print could spark a 1-2% relief rally. Options structure (straddle or put spread) allows defined-risk capture of the binary outcome.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI release July 14, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET; Fed Chair Warsh Congressional testimony July 14; FOMC meeting July 28-29.
ideas.edge: Market pricing only 25% July hike probability — a hot CPI print could reprice this sharply higher with outsized QQQ downside; soft headline could spark 1-2% rally given S&P near all-time highs at ~7,500. Short-dated straddle or put spread captures binary with defined max loss.
ideas.bull_case: Headline CPI comes in soft/negative on energy base effects, market focuses on relief narrative, QQQ rallies 1-2%+ into a tech-heavy earnings week with MSFT and AAPL on deck for late July.
ideas.bear_case: Sticky core CPI at or above 2.9% combined with hawkish Warsh testimony reprices September hike odds above 61%, compressing Nasdaq-100 growth multiples from near all-time-high levels with limited valuation cushion.
